_comment: Tune these first before touching scanner logic. Start loose, tighten once you see how much noise comes through.

_base_notes.min_volume: Absolute contract volume floor. Below this, ratios are meaningless (10 vol / 2 OI = 5x ratio but it's nothing).
_base_notes.min_vol_oi_ratio: Today's volume as a multiple of existing open interest. 3x+ means most of today's activity is new positioning, not rolls.
_base_notes.min_notional: volume * last_price * 100. Filters out small retail-sized clutter.
_base_notes.min_dte: Excludes 0DTE/1DTE contracts. Daily-expiry products reset OI structurally every day, so vol/OI ratio is meaningless there -- it flags normal mechanics as 'unusual'. This was the main noise source in the first run.
_base_notes.max_dte: Days to expiration cap. Short-dated positioning suggests a near-term catalyst thesis.
_base_notes.otm_only: If true, only flags out-of-the-money contracts (classic directional bet pattern).

_index_tickers_note: These have daily expirations and dramatically higher baseline volume than single names. They get their own (stricter) thresholds below instead of being compared against AAPL/TSLA-style thresholds.

_index_overrides_notes.why_higher: SPY/QQQ single-leg volume in the tens of thousands is routine. These thresholds are set high enough that a hit should actually stand out relative to typical index flow, not just reflect the product's baseline liquidity.
_index_overrides_notes.min_dte_5: Even non-0/1DTE weeklies on indices roll fast; pushing the floor to 5 days filters out most mechanical noise while still catching real positioning.

_earnings_filter_mode_note: Cross-references each ticker's next confirmed/estimated earnings date against contract expiry. 'flag_only' annotates every row with whether earnings falls before expiry but does not filter anything out (default -- a non-earnings flag can still be real, e.g. unscheduled news, dealer flow, M&A rumor). Set to 'require' to drop any contract whose expiry is before the next known earnings date. Index tickers (SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA) are marked N/A since they have no single-company earnings date.
_otm_tiers_note: Classifies how far OTM a contract is (% distance strike vs spot). Added after manually checking the 5 earnings-before-expiry hits from the v1.2 run: TSLA/GOOGL strikes were within the market's own implied earnings move (plausible informed positioning), while MSFT (+15.6%) and META (+25.6%) needed moves far beyond any realistic earnings reaction -- more consistent with cheap lottery-ticket retail speculation than real size behind a thesis. Tiers: near_money (<=5%), moderate (<=10%), aggressive (<=20%), lottery (>20%). This does not filter anything out by default -- it's used to re-rank results so lottery-tier bets sort below plausible ones.
